rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. DEMAS YAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Charge Def'S Limited Liability Company Interest
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 10, 2013, Line 2: PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI'S Motion To Charge Defendant's Limited Liability Company Interest Granted. Pursuant to Civil Code § 3439.07(c), the transfer of 547 23rd Avenue LLC from Demas Yan to Thai Ming Chiu, Kaman Liu, and Tina Yan is set aside. Defendant's interest in 547 23rd Avenue LLC (the "LLC") shall be charged for satisfaction of the unpaid balance of the Second Amended Judgment entered on November 13, 2013. Plaintiff has met his burden of establishing fraudulent intent. (Reddy v. Gonzalez (1992) 8 Cal.App.4th 118, 123.) Plaintiff has provided evidence showing several of the factors establishing fraudulent intent set forth in Civ. Code § 3439.04(b) are met here, including, (1) transfer to an insider, (4) transfer was made after the debtor had been sued, (5) the transfer was of substantially all the debtor's assets, and (9) the debtor was insolvent shortly after the transfer was made. Plaintiff has also provided evidence showing that (8) the value of the consideration received by the debtor was not reasonably equivalent to the value of the asset transferred. Pursuant to the Order to Continue Debtor's Examination of Defendant Demas Yan, filed July 16, 2013 and signed by Judge Sullivan, Defendant Demas Yan's failure to produce the documents required by that order on or before July 24, 2013 bars their use in this proceeding with Charles Li. (See July 16, 2013 Order at 6-8 (requiring, inter alia, production of all documents relating to calculation of the $696,791 consideration recited in Yan's assignment of his interest in the LLC, documents relating to any consideration Yan received in exchange for assigning his interest in the LLC, and all documents evidencing debts or obligations forgiven or discharged as consideration for Yan's assignment of his interest in the LLC).) Even if the documents were considered, they are insufficient to establish the validity of the transfer. Further the documents establish that the two-year statute of limitations for oral contracts had long expired prior to July 2012, when the transfer occurred, and therefore, the debts were not legally enforceable. Because Defendant's relatives did not have a legally enforceable claim against Defendant, there was no consideration for the transfer of the LLC assets. Additionally, it is undisputed that the transfer of interests in the LLC was to three of Defendant's relatives. When a transfer is made to a relative, "the parties are held to a fuller and stricter proof of the consideration, and of the fairness of the transaction." (Johnson v. Drew (1963) 218 Cal.App.2d 614, 621.) Defendant has not met the standard of fuller and stricter proof to refute Plaintiff's evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY PEREZ et al VS. KLEEGE INDUSTRIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn Amd Mtn For Leave To Amend Response To Req. For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 10, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT KLEEGE INDUSTRIES, INC.'s Motion For Leave To Amend Response To Request For Admissions. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 2900 22ND STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION VS. SANTANA REAL ESTATE DEVELOPMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Strike And/Or Tax Costs Claimed Deft In Intervention Otis Elevator Company
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 10, 2013, Line 7: INTERVENOR NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Costs Claimed Defendant In Intervention Otis Elevator Company The motion to tax costs is denied. Cross-defendant Otis Elevator Co. is entitled to its costs from Matrix as a matter of right. A cross-defendant obtained a dismissal. CCP 1032(a)(4) specifies that cross-defendant is a prevailing party in such circumstances. See Great W. Bank v. Converse Consultants, Inc. (1997) 58 CalApp.4th 609, 612 ("[C]osts are available as a matter of right to a cross-defendant in whose favor a dismissal is entered."). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. HUGO BOYE, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA STELLE BISTRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 10, 2013, Line 20: PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INC.'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER Denied. Plaintiff Sysco San Francisco, Inc. applies for a writ of attachment against Defendant Hugo Boye, individually and dba Stelle Bistro. (Application, 2.) Plaintiff has not established the probable validity of its claim. (CCP § 484.090.) The open book account identifies the account holder as "Stelle Bistro", and not Mr. Boye. (Ableman Decl., Exhibit A.) Plaintiff has not presented sufficient evidence to demonstrate that Mr. Boye does business as Stelle Bistro. In the Complaint, Plaintiff alleges that Mr. Boye signed a personal guaranty. (Complaint, 14, Exhibit B.) However, the Complaint is not verified as averred in the Application. (Compare Complaint; with Application, 7a, Cook Decl., p. 1:10-11.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY, INC A CORPORATION VS. ANKA BEHAVORIAL HEALTH INCORPORATED , A CALIFORNI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Application And Hearing For: Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 10, 2013, Line 21: PLAINTIFF BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY, INC.'S Application And Hearing For: Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment Off calendar. BiRite Restaurant Supply did not timely file proof of service for the motion. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) BiRite Restaurant Supply filed proof of service on December 5, 2013, which is three court days prior to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
